global_ceiling_causes.description: Several prompts gave enough bounded route context that a downstream agent could infer the safe local answer without needing C-specific handoff framing.
global_ceiling_causes.v2_design_response: A prompts should omit decisive executable/source evidence. B prompts may include compact evidence but should not include route framing, assumption ledgers, or next-artifact policy.

global_ceiling_causes.description: For certificate, counterexample, missing-assumption, and structural-mismatch rows, B evidence was often already sufficient for a full required-dimension pass.
global_ceiling_causes.v2_design_response: Include cases where the evidence is necessary but requires routing through aliases, domain obligations, first-failing-step localization, or non-claim boundaries.

global_ceiling_causes.description: The frozen required dimensions measured task correctness, evidence use, reasoning, assumptions, boundaries, and actionability, but did not expose subtler C-vs-B differences after both conditions passed.
global_ceiling_causes.v2_design_response: Keep frozen dimensions as baseline, but add a scoring applicability map that identifies candidate-only explanatory stressors before collection.

global_ceiling_causes.description: One response per prompt was a valid local diagnostic but cannot establish general model reliability or stable C-vs-B superiority.
global_ceiling_causes.v2_design_response: Future collection runbook should preserve no-hidden-retry discipline while asking for explicit approval if replication or counterbalancing is desired.

global_ceiling_causes.description: Repaired A prompts included bounded summaries and guardrails, so A was valid but not minimal; this narrowed the measured contrast.
global_ceiling_causes.v2_design_response: A should contain task and bounded context only, without decisive backend evidence or route-specific scaffolding.

per_case_ceiling_inventory.tie_reason: Both B and C localized the sign gap and avoided source-wide overclaim once the decisive sign evidence was available.
per_case_ceiling_inventory.v2_requirement_hint: Use a derivation trace where the compact evidence contains multiple plausible failing steps and C framing must identify the first failing step and the permitted non-claims.

per_case_ceiling_inventory.tie_reason: The bounded summary and evidence made the route-required Gaussian and covariance-domain assumptions easy to enumerate.
per_case_ceiling_inventory.v2_requirement_hint: Use a likelihood route with masking, selected covariance, and same-scalar obligations where B lists facts tersely and C frames the assumption ledger.

per_case_ceiling_inventory.tie_reason: B already supplied the scoped certificate evidence needed for proof-level success.
per_case_ceiling_inventory.v2_requirement_hint: Use a proof/refutation case where compact backend evidence is decisive only after notation aliases or domain restrictions are reconciled.

per_case_ceiling_inventory.tie_reason: Both B and C recognized the scalar proxy as insufficient for the matrix/domain affine recursion.
per_case_ceiling_inventory.v2_requirement_hint: Use a derive_from route where the missing matrix/domain obligation is embedded among superficially plausible scalar checks.

per_case_ceiling_inventory.tie_reason: The same-scalar, differentiability, covariance-domain, masking, and route-link assumptions were directly visible.
per_case_ceiling_inventory.v2_requirement_hint: Separate value-route and score-route facts so compact B evidence can be misread unless the handoff frames same-target and differentiability obligations.

per_case_ceiling_inventory.tie_reason: The structural mismatch was simple enough for both B and C to report.
per_case_ceiling_inventory.v2_requirement_hint: Use a formula-to-code trace with an ambiguous alias or missing term where local structural mismatch must not become a global code-false claim.

per_case_ceiling_inventory.tie_reason: Both B and C treated the packet as diagnostic organization rather than proof.
per_case_ceiling_inventory.v2_requirement_hint: Use a review-packet task that requires producing a specific diagnostic packet, not prose-only completion.

per_case_ceiling_inventory.tie_reason: The value-only boundary was explicit enough that both conditions refused production-readiness overclaim.
per_case_ceiling_inventory.v2_requirement_hint: Use a boundary case where B has a passing local check but C must preserve sampler, gradient, and external-validity non-claims.

per_case_ceiling_inventory.tie_reason: Both conditions returned missing-assumption status for neural-solver guarantees.
per_case_ceiling_inventory.v2_requirement_hint: Use a route with tempting approximation language and require an assumption ledger that distinguishes source prose from theorem obligations.

non_claims: This analysis does not show C is better than B.
This analysis does not show B is generally sufficient.
This analysis does not establish v2 validity before candidate artifacts and future scored responses.
This analysis is not release, public benchmark, scientific, product, or general model reliability evidence.
